title: Fresh Food - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Fresh Food 22 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients sh Food Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BIENESTAR MERCADO BIENESTAR MERCADO FOOD PANTRY AND FREE FOOD MARKET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a mercado, that provides a wide assortment of fresh fruits and vegetables to community members. 6736 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 1st and 3rd Wednesday of the month 9am-11am (503) 988-3509 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES HARVEST SHARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh produce and bread. Items may vary. 207 NE Park Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 426-9411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEXTER FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes, seasonal produce, and a clothing closet. 38925 Dexter Road Dexter, OR 97431 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: 1st and 3rd Tuesday of the month 1pm-6pm Email (541) 937-4229 Get Directions More Details FARMERS MARKET FUND DOUBLE UP FOOD BUCKS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Allows people to double SNAP dollars to spend on fresh produce at participating farmers markets, CSAs (community-supported agriculture), farm stands, and grocery stores. Website Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: SNAP recipients Hours: Varies Email (503) 208-4495 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FARMERS MARKET FUND PRODUCE MATCH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF If a person receives cash benefits on their Oregon Trail card rather than SNAP (food) benefits, they are eligible to participate in the Produce Match program. This program allows people to double SNAP cash dollars to spend on fresh produce at participating farmers markets. Website Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: EBT Cash recipients Hours: Varies Email (503) 208-4495 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOOD ROOTS COMMUNITY CHURCH FOOD AND BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly emergency food box including bread and pastries. Clothing closet and personal care items are available. Provides a community garden that is open to the public to pick produce. 1908 SE Courtney Road Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food pantry: Saturday 10am-noon Community Garden: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Clothing and personal items available Saturdays 8:30am-11:30am Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details HALFWAY LIONS CLUB HALFWAY LIONS-PANHANDLE FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a monthly food box and a fresh food distribution program. 235 Lions Street Halfway, OR 97834 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Food Pantry: 3rd Monday of the month noon-1pm Fresh Alliance: 1st Monday of the month noon-1pm Email (541) 742-4025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SENIOR AND COMMUNITY CENTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides utility assistance for gas, electric, and heating fuel bills; motel vouchers; one-time gas vouchers; transportation passes; food pantry (visit twice per month); Fresh Alliance program (provides fresh produce, dairy, and bread products); weatherization services; and blood pressure screenings. Offers snack kits. Assistive medical equipment and mobility aids also available. 17 S Alder Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: -Food pantry: Households at or below 300% federal poverty level. No proof of income is required. -Fresh Alliance: Unrestricted -Snack kits for those looking to keep moving through the community -Motel vouchers: Homeless individuals requiring one night's stay -Gas vouchers: Must be stuck and looking to continue to move through the community -Transportation passes: Must have no ties to the community and looking to continue to move through the community -Energy Assistance: Household must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Fresh Alliance: Monday 10am-10:30am Food pantry: Wednesday 10am-1pm, Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (541) 573-6024 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOSEPHINE COUNTY FARM COLLECTIVE EMERGENCY FOOD ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly emergency food box. Options include vegetarian, eggs, meat, and vegetables, and shelf-stable items only. Accepts food donations. 485 Daily Lane Grants Pass, OR 97527 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food box requests: Sunday 6am-Wednesday 6am Farm stand pick up: November-April Tuesday 3pm-6pm, May-October 4pm-7pm, Wednesday 8am-5pm locations vary Email (503) 304-3754 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOU'S LOAFS TAKE HOME MEALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers prepackaged take-home meals for pickup. 1254 N 3rd Street Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 3pm Email (541) 727-7275 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MADRAS COMMUNITY FOOD PANTRY FOOD PANTRY AND FREE FOOD MARKET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry with dairy, baked goods, produce, meat, and perishable items. Additional fresh food is available from food retrieved from grocery stores twice a month as part of the Fresh Alliance program. 370 SW Culver Highway Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Pantry: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm, 3rd Wednesday of the month 5pm-6pm (April-October) Free Food Market: 2nd and 4th Wednesday, sign up 4pm and doors open 4:30pm until all food has been distributed Email (541) 325-1747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details METOLIUS FRIENDS COMMUNITY CHURCH METOLIUS FRIENDS FOOD PANTRY AND FREE FOOD MARKET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and fresh food market. 575 Hood Avenue Metolius, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Friday 4pm-6pm, closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day Free Food Market: 1st Friday of the month 4:30pm-5:30pm (541) 546-4974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF MARION, POLK, AND YAMHILL COUNTIES MCMINNVILLE CHILD WELFARE, SELF-SUFFICIENCY, AND VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION OFFICE HARVEST TO HOME Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh produce to low income individuals and families. 368 NE Norton Lane McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Last Thursday of the month 2pm-3pm Email (503) 472-0311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRY AND MEAL LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The food pantry locator is an online tool to search for food assistance, community meal sites, and other food distribution programs. The Oregon Food Bank distributes food to local organizations who then distribute to the public. Website Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY GRESHAM CORPS FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. Households may pick up a food box up to 12 times per year. Visits must be 30 days apart. 473 SE 194th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-11:45am Email (503) 661-8972 413 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SENIOR CENTERS BAKER COUNTY BAKER COUNTY SENIOR CENTER FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. Participants can visit once per month. Also offers fresh produce and bread. 2810 Cedar Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Food Pantry: Must meet USDA income limits Harvest Share: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Thursday 9am-11am Harvest Share: Thursday 10:30am-noon (541) 523-6591 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SENIOR CENTERS UNION COUNTY UNION COUNTY SENIOR CENTER FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh produce and bread product program. Also offers a food pantry with easy-to-prepare items. 1504 N Albany Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Food Pantry: Household income must be at or below 300% federal poverty level and have limited or no access to cooking facilities Harvest Share: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1:30pm-4:30pm Harvest Share: Wednesday 8am, Friday 1:30pm (until capacity is reached) (541) 963-7532 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE DOOR THE DOOR AT LA PINE FREE FOOD MARKET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food market that may include produce, dairy, bread and meat. 16430 3rd Street La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 3rd Monday of the month 2:30pm-4pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE DOOR THE DOOR AT THREE RIVERS FREE FOOD MARKET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food market that may include produce, dairy, bread and meat. 56885 Enterprise Drive Sunriver, OR 97707 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 4th Friday of the month, sign up at 2:30pm, distribution 2:40pm-until capacity is reached Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNIVERSITY PARK ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly food pantry and monthly free food market to those in need. Individuals are required to bring their own bags or boxes to transport items. 4007 N Alaska Street Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Food Pantry: Wednesday 5pm-7pm, while supplies last Free Food Market: 1st Friday of the month 11am-noon Closed on Christmas Eve and New Years Eve (503) 397-4389 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WINDING WATERS CLINIC ENTERPRISE MAIN CLINIC HARVEST SHARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh produce and bread products. Items may vary. 603 Medical Parkway Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours (541) 426-4052 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WINDING WATERS CLINIC JOSEPH CLINIC HARVEST SHARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh produce and bread products. Items may vary. 401 N Main Street Joseph, OR 97846 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 426-4052 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP REGIONAL FOOD BANK HARVEST TO HOME Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, surplus produce, and perishable items. 410 E 3rd Street Beulah Park Yamhill, OR 97148 Eligibility: Household income must be at or below 300% of the federal poverty level, or the household must receive SNAP (food stamps), TANF, SSI, or LIHEAP Hours: 2nd and 4th Thursday of the month 1pm-2:30pm (503) 472-0457 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BIENESTAR MERCADO BIENESTAR MERCADO FOOD PANTRY AND FREE FOOD MARKET BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES HARVEST SHARE DEXTER FOOD PANTRY DEXTER FOOD PANTRY FARMERS MARKET FUND DOUBLE UP FOOD BUCKS GOOD ROOTS COMMUNITY CHURCH FOOD AND BASIC NEEDS HALFWAY LIONS CLUB HALFWAY LIONS-PANHANDLE FOOD BANK HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SENIOR AND COMMUNITY CENTER SERVICES JOSEPHINE COUNTY FARM COLLECTIVE EMERGENCY FOOD ASSISTANCE LOU'S LOAFS TAKE HOME MEALS MADRAS COMMUNITY FOOD PANTRY FOOD PANTRY AND FREE FOOD MARKET METOLIUS FRIENDS COMMUNITY CHURCH METOLIUS FRIENDS FOOD PANTRY AND FREE FOOD MARKET OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF MARION, POLK, AND YAMHILL COUNTIES HARVEST TO HOME SALVATION ARMY GRESHAM CORPS FOOD PANTRY SENIOR CENTERS BAKER COUNTY FOOD PANTRY SENIOR CENTERS UNION COUNTY FOOD PANTRY THE DOOR FREE FOOD MARKET THE DOOR FREE FOOD MARKET UNIVERSITY PARK ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES FOOD PANTRY WINDING WATERS CLINIC HARVEST SHARE WINDING WATERS CLINIC HARVEST SHARE YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP REGIONAL FOOD BANK HARVEST TO HOME
